question_text: A washing machine has an efficiency of 65%. If it uses 5000 J of electrical energy in one wash cycle, calculate (a) the useful energy output and (b) the energy wasted as heat and sound.
explanation: For (a): useful energy = efficiency × total input energy = 0.65 × 5000 J = 3250 J. For (b): wasted energy = total input energy − useful energy = 5000 − 3250 = 1750 J. This shows that even 'efficient' appliances still waste significant energy, which is why choosing efficient models and maintaining them properly matters for reducing household energy use.
